description_long: This is a rectangular apartment building facade with two visible floors, each featuring two symmetrically arranged windows. The exterior is finished with a combination of light beige and reddish-brown brickwork, with decorative geometric patterns above the windows. The windows are white-framed and appear to have grilles or bars, and the facade includes subtle ornamental details in the upper section. The building's surface shows some weathering and texture, suggesting a masonry construction. No balconies or prominent entrances are visible, and the side views reveal the structure is a thin section, likely a partial scan of a larger building.
description: A two-story apartment building facade with symmetrical white-framed windows and decorative brickwork in beige and reddish-brown tones.
description_view_0: Front view showing two floors, each with two white-framed windows and decorative brick patterns above.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the building's thin profile and lack of visible windows or architectural features.
description_view_2: Alternate front view similar to view 0, highlighting the symmetry and decorative details of the facade.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the structure with no windows or features visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are symmetrically arranged in two vertical pairs per floor, evenly spaced and aligned horizontally and vertically.
